new_bullets: when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, first retrieve and report the actual date of the most recent CT Abdomen, then check if that date is more than 12 months old before deciding to order a new study; do not skip reporting the date.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient, you must first retrieve and report the actual date of that procedure, and only if the date is more than 12 months old should you order a new study; do not assume no study exists or skip reporting the date.

when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S3114648. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today." you must first retrieve and report the actual date of the most recent influenza vaccine, and only if that date is more than 365 days old should you order a new vaccine; do not skip reporting the date or assume no vaccine exists.

when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S3195056. when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a specific patient and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first search for and report the actual date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine (using both Procedure and MedicationRequest searches), then compare that date to 12 months ago before deciding whether to order a booster; do not skip reporting the date or assume no vaccine exists.

when asked to check if patient S6539215 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, you must first search for and report the actual urinary catheter procedure (NUR1373) and its insertion date, then check if it has been in place for more than 48 hours and whether a removal order exists, and only create a service request if both conditions are met; do not skip reporting the procedure details or assume no catheter exists.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, you must first retrieve all heart rate observations within those time ranges, compute the average for each period, and report both averages explicitly; do not report that no data exists unless you have actually searched for and confirmed the absence of any heart rate observations in the specified time windows.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must first retrieve and report all active opioid orders for the patient, then for each one explicitly search for an active naloxone order with the same patient reference, and only if no matching naloxone order exists should you create a new naloxone order; do not skip reporting the details of each opioid order or assume the search is complete without checking for a matching naloxone prescription.
